subject: LU-3322 ko2iblnd: Support different configs between systems

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Amir I just posted in the LU-3322 ticket my results. I have a actually setup that requires map_on_demand to be different on one of my client clusters.

Patch Set 4:

Issac can you take a look at the patch. Thank you.

Patch Set 4: -Code-Review

Several more patches will land soon that will break this as well.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

When I set map_on_demand explicitly to the default value on my server nodes this worked!!!!! Thank you.

Patch Set 4:

So far my testing of this feature has been unsuccessful. Amir did you have success?

Patch Set 4:

I see some misconceptions here. First you don't need to set map_on_demand by default with this patch. You do need to set it if you are communication with a node that does have it set. Second not setting map_on_demand does not mean FMR pools are not allocated. The first time I brought up the mlx5 on the Power8 node that first thing it did was it reported FMR pool allocation failure since it is not supported by this driver. This is without map_on_demand being set. I plan to test the various combos we can have and report back to you on how the patch behaves

Patch Set 4:

Hi Isaac,

Did the comments from Jeremy address your questions?

(2 comments)

Sorry for the delay. I had a couple of questions posted inline. It'd be very helpful if you can briefly describe how the patch handles peers with different peer_credits/map_on_demand. Thank you!

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

I want to carefully review this patch but I can't get to it until next Monday or Tuesday. Temporary -1 just so the gatekeeper won't merge it - sorry about that. I'll make sure to review it early next week.

Patch Set 4:

Sorry I missed the comments in LU-3322. You suggested to set map_on_demand=256 on servers. Can you elaborate why it's needed for this patch? I thought it'd enable peers with different map_on_demand values to connect, but now it seemed map_on_demand can't be 0 for this patch to work.

With map_on_demand=0, normally we don't have to create the PMR/FMR pools which is quite a save.

Patch Set 4:

(3 comments)

More comments:

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Hi Jeremy, seems that this patch will always reject connecting request which has different parameters, and rely on remote side to retry with values piggybacked by rejecting reply. I think this will not allow old version client to connect to new server with map_on_demand, and if server never try to connect to old client, then old client will never be able to connect to server? I think allow old versions to connect to new version  should be major goal of this patch?
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

It will be good to add an overview of how the different values for peer_credits and map_on_demand are actually excahnged between the nodes.  A description of the protocol in the commit message.  Makes it easier for readers to get the idea quicker.

Patch Set 4:

James, I usually depend on the Maloo testing.  Can you share the reason for failure in your testing?

Patch Set 8:

Also forgot to mention that peer_credits for the mlx5 driver is limited to 16 whereas mlx4 driver can go as high as 128.

Patch Set 8:

Remove peer_credit will make this patch worthless. With the removal of PMR the mlx5 driver no longer can be managed using map_on_demand which leaves altering peer_credits to make it work with a mlx4 system on the other end. I under it will make it racy but we really need to make the ko2iblnd driver really work with the mlx5 driver.

(6 comments)

I'd suggest to remove peer credits from this patch, it could be more complex than this because per-peer router buffer credits could rely on peer credits of o2iblnd, it may have some potential problem (we met some very complex router deadlock because of credits system which was difficult to fix)

Please see the comments in LU-3322 and if that is sufficient let me know so we can push the path along.  If there isn't anything else further with the patch I can just address the documentation bug.

Patch Set 9:

This patch needs to retain the peer_credits changes.  The whole reason I started down this path 3 years ago was due to the lack of flexibility in the ko2iblnd current configuration.  There may be potential for difficult deadlock problems but it this is an enhancement and often times they include bugs.

parents.subject: LU-6584 osd: prevent int type overflow in osd_read_prep()

commit.subject: LU-3322 ko2iblnd: Support different configs between systems
commit.message:
LU-3322 ko2iblnd: Support different configs between systems

This patch adds suppoort for ko2iblnd to have different values for
peer_credits and map_on_demand between systems.

Signed-off-by: Jeremy Filizetti <jeremy.filizetti@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Idfe5acdfdde5c2185488b92c96d7a83f1705a556
